task_completion.description: The messy sleep tracking data is opened and visible in LibreOffice Calc.
task_completion.visual_evidence: LibreOffice Calc window is open displaying columns like Date, Bedtime, Wake Time, Quality Score, Caffeine, Screen Time, and Exercise.

task_completion.description: Correlations between sleep quality and factors such as caffeine, screen time, and exercise are calculated using formulas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Summary tables or cells showing average sleep quality scores grouped by caffeine intake, exercise, or screen time. The formula bar should ideally show functions like AVERAGEIF being used.

task_completion.description: The optimal bedtime is determined and explicitly identified using formulas.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific cell, section, or summary table that identifies the 'Optimal Bedtime' based on the highest sleep quality or longest duration. Formula usage should be evident in the formula bar when these cells are selected.

task_completion.description: Conditional formatting is applied to the spreadsheet to highlight patterns or optimal times.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells in the spreadsheet have automatic color-coding (e.g., green for high quality/optimal bedtime, red for low quality). The conditional formatting dialog might be visible in some screenshots.

task_completion.description: The analysis results are saved to the specified path as an Excel (.xlsx) file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Calc window title bar shows 'sleep_analysis.xlsx', or a 'Save As' dialog is visible showing the file being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/sleep_analysis.xlsx'.

integrity.description: The agent used the LibreOffice Calc GUI to perform the analysis rather than a terminal script.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the LibreOffice Calc interface, such as selecting cells, typing formulas in the formula bar, or using menu options.

integrity.description: The agent used actual spreadsheet formulas rather than manually typing in hardcoded answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar (fx) shows functions (e.g., =AVERAGEIF, =IF, time arithmetic) rather than static text or numbers for the calculated cells.

integrity.description: The agent applied actual conditional formatting rules rather than manually changing cell background colors.
integrity.visual_evidence: The conditional formatting dialog box is seen, or the color coding perfectly aligns with cell values in a way that implies a rule was applied to a range.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The input data has 21 rows and 8 columns with messy formats (mixed 12h/24h times, inconsistent capitalization like Yes/YES/no). A complete solution typically includes: 1) Sleep duration time arithmetic, 2) Quality categorization IF formulas, 3) Conditional formatting, 4) AVERAGEIF correlation formulas for caffeine/screen time/exercise vs quality, 5) Optimal bedtime analysis, and 6) At least 10 total formulas used. The final file must be saved exactly as sleep_analysis.xlsx.
